questions.q: U. R. Rao is most popularly remembered by which nickname in India?
options: The Father of the Indian Bomb
questions.explanation: U. R. Rao is widely called 'The Satellite Man of India' for leading the development of India's first satellites.

questions.q: U. R. Rao led the team that built which spacecraft, India's first satellite?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata, launched in 1975, was India's first satellite and was developed under U. R. Rao's leadership.

questions.q: In which field of science did U. R. Rao primarily work?
questions.explanation: U. R. Rao was a space scientist who worked on cosmic rays, satellites and India's space programme.

questions.q: U. R. Rao served as the head of which major Indian space organisation?
questions.explanation: U. R. Rao served as Chairman of the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO).

questions.q: What was U. R. Rao's role within the Indian space programme?
options: A satellite scientist and ISRO leader
questions.explanation: U. R. Rao was a satellite scientist who later led ISRO as its chairman.

questions.q: The satellite Aryabhata, built under U. R. Rao, was named after which figure?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata was named after the 5th-century Indian astronomer and mathematician Aryabhata.

questions.q: U. R. Rao is celebrated mainly for advancing which kind of technology in India?
questions.explanation: He pioneered satellite design and construction in India, earning the title 'Satellite Man'.

questions.q: Which country's space programme did U. R. Rao help build?
questions.explanation: U. R. Rao was a central figure in building India's space programme through ISRO.

questions.q: What does the abbreviation 'U. R.' in U. R. Rao stand for?
questions.explanation: His full name was Udupi Ramachandra Rao.

questions.q: U. R. Rao worked closely with which person regarded as the father of the Indian space programme?
questions.explanation: U. R. Rao was mentored by and worked under Vikram Sarabhai, the father of India's space programme.

questions.q: In which year was Aryabhata, India's first satellite, launched?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata was launched on 19 April 1975.

questions.q: Aryabhata, built under U. R. Rao, was placed into orbit by a launch vehicle from which country?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata was launched by a Soviet Kosmos-3M rocket under an India-USSR agreement, as India did not yet have its own orbital launcher.

questions.q: Before working on satellites, U. R. Rao did notable early research in which area under Vikram Sarabhai?
questions.explanation: U. R. Rao began his research career studying cosmic rays under Vikram Sarabhai at the Physical Research Laboratory.

questions.q: Early in his career, U. R. Rao analysed data from which kind of American space missions?
questions.explanation: He worked in the United States on cosmic-ray and solar-wind experiments aboard Pioneer and other interplanetary spacecraft.

questions.q: Which research institution, associated with Vikram Sarabhai in Ahmedabad, was central to U. R. Rao's early scientific work?
questions.explanation: U. R. Rao did much of his early cosmic-ray research at the Physical Research Laboratory (PRL) in Ahmedabad.

questions.q: Which series of Indian Earth-observation satellites was developed during U. R. Rao's leadership at ISRO?
questions.explanation: The Indian Remote Sensing (IRS) satellite series advanced significantly under U. R. Rao's direction at ISRO.

questions.q: U. R. Rao was the first director of which ISRO centre, today named in his honour?
options: The ISRO Satellite Centre (now U. R. Rao Satellite Centre)
questions.explanation: He set up and led the satellite-building centre in Bengaluru, later renamed the U. R. Rao Satellite Centre in his honour.

questions.q: U. R. Rao served as Chairman of ISRO during which approximate period?
questions.explanation: U. R. Rao was Chairman of ISRO from 1984 to 1994.

questions.q: While U. R. Rao chaired ISRO, work advanced on which indigenous satellite launch vehicle programme?
questions.explanation: The development and first flight of the medium-lift PSLV took place during U. R. Rao's tenure as ISRO chairman; the other rockets listed are foreign vehicles.

questions.q: Which high civilian honour was awarded to U. R. Rao by the Government of India?
questions.explanation: U. R. Rao was awarded the Padma Vibhushan, one of India's highest civilian honours.

questions.q: In what decade did U. R. Rao pass away?
questions.explanation: U. R. Rao died in 2017, in the 2010s.

questions.q: Besides remote sensing, ISRO under U. R. Rao expanded which other application-focused satellite system?
questions.explanation: The INSAT communication and meteorological satellite system grew substantially during his leadership at ISRO.

questions.q: U. R. Rao earned his doctoral degree under the guidance of which Indian scientist?
questions.explanation: U. R. Rao completed his PhD with Vikram Sarabhai as his doctoral advisor.

questions.q: What was a notable international recognition received by U. R. Rao related to space exploration history?
options: Induction into the Satellite Hall of Fame / IAF Hall of Fame
questions.explanation: U. R. Rao received international space honours and was the first Indian inducted into the Satellite Hall of Fame, and later the IAF Hall of Fame.

questions.q: Aryabhata, built under U. R. Rao, carried experiments primarily in which scientific domains?
options: X-ray astronomy, aeronomics and solar physics
questions.explanation: Aryabhata carried scientific payloads for X-ray astronomy, aeronomics and solar physics experiments.

questions.q: U. R. Rao was born in 1932 in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: U. R. Rao was born in 1932 in Adamaru, in the Udupi region of present-day Karnataka.

questions.q: Which two early Indian Earth-observation satellites, sent up in the late 1970s and early 1980s, were among those developed in U. R. Rao's programme?
questions.explanation: The Bhaskara-1 (1979) and Bhaskara-2 (1981) experimental Earth-observation satellites came out of the satellite programme he led.

questions.q: U. R. Rao also chaired the governing council of which Indian space policy/promotion body in his later career?
options: The Physical Research Laboratory and related space committees
questions.explanation: After his ISRO chairmanship, U. R. Rao led and advised major Indian space-science bodies, including chairing the governing council of the Physical Research Laboratory.

questions.q: The renaming of the ISRO Satellite Centre to the 'U. R. Rao Satellite Centre' took effect in which year, shortly after his death?
questions.explanation: The ISRO Satellite Centre in Bengaluru was renamed the U. R. Rao Satellite Centre with effect from 2 April 2018, the year after he died in 2017.

questions.q: Which statement best captures U. R. Rao's lasting significance to Indian science?
options: He invented the first Indian computer
He pioneered indigenous satellite design and led India into the space age
He discovered a new chemical element
He was India's first astronaut in orbit
questions.explanation: U. R. Rao's enduring legacy is establishing indigenous satellite technology in India, beginning with Aryabhata, and steering ISRO's growth as its chairman.
